summary.why: User reframed the gate's purpose as capturing live in-session reasoning, accepting misses outside the session — the current gate doesn't distinguish, so it fires on foreign/stale work that can't be productively documented. Bumped timbers-vlh from P3 to P1 and authored a plan doc to make the gate provenance-aware. Got the draft reviewed by three independent reviewers (pragmatist, correctness-skeptic, agent-UX advocate) and refined accordingly. Committing the refined plan so implementation can start from a definite spec.
summary.how: Wrote docs/plans/2026-06-01-cross-agent-debt-classification.md following the existing plan-doc convention (Status/Date/Target version header, design principles, scope/out-of-scope, phases, acceptance criteria). Spawned three Opus subagents in parallel with focused lenses: pragmatist cut 4 items (status breakdown, default footer, --include-foreign, session-author) and shrunk the tally; correctness-skeptic found the load-bearing AuthorDate-vs-CommitDate issue (rebase preserves AuthorDate so the heuristic would silently misclassify in-session work) plus several edge-case acceptance tests; agent-UX advocate identified the pending.count semantic flip as the single most important UX decision (agents anchor on that field), pushed 4h → 24h default, and flagged the silent same-author stale-skip as the unrecoverable failure mode. Synthesized into a Decided-in-Review section so the rationale is durable. Bead updated to P1 with the refined scope summary.

notes: Resolved one tension explicitly: pragmatist wanted to cut the .timbersignore session-window directive (constant + tally); agent-UX wanted per-repo tuning. Kept the directive because correctness-skeptic's rebase/amend finding makes window correctness load-bearing and the 24h default may be wrong for some workflows — per-repo override is cheap insurance. Did NOT add a stale-self warning to the gate itself (would defeat the lenience reframe); moved it to a post-commit stderr note that doesn't block. Cut --include-foreign entirely from v0.23.0 — pragmatist correctly observed it's for a backfill workflow that doesn't yet have a real user. Tally shrunk to ~30 commits in this repo because the only open question is 'does email-mismatch alone get us to zero false-positives' — that's a 10-min shell script, not a phase. Phase 1 (tally) is gating before any implementation lands.
